artifact_name: Claim-Boundary and Buyer-Facing Integrity Review v0.1
status: internal claim-boundary audit, not validated benchmark
allowed_claims: This is a structured internal schema for generating adversarial test cases against AI-system scaffolds, designed to surface specific known failure modes.
The schema enforces structural discipline: every case requires a non-empty conditional failure_column, a controlled category, and a ten-dimension scoring structure with explicit pass/fail conditions.
The packet explicitly excludes named-model/vendor claims, validated-performance claims, and live-deployment instructions at the schema level.
This is a draft schema for generating a test suite, not itself a completed suite, result, or finding.
weak_claims: That the controlled categories constitute a comprehensive or validated taxonomy of failure modes.
That the ten review dimensions are the right or complete dimensions for evaluating scaffold reliability.
That buyer-safe language substitutions fully neutralize the underlying risk category rather than just reducing buyer alarm.
forbidden_claims: That this schema or any suite generated from it constitutes a benchmark, certification, compliance product, audit, or security tool.
That a generated v0.5 suite would validate, certify, or guarantee the safety, reliability, or compliance posture of any AI system tested against it.
That passing or failing cases generated from this schema indicates real-world deployment readiness.
That this artifact has been validated, peer-reviewed, or independently audited.
That this is HR, security, healthcare-compliance, or legal-compliance tooling.

claims_requiring_evidence.claim: The generated suite meaningfully predicts real-world scaffold failure rates.
claims_requiring_evidence.evidence_needed: Run generated suite against real deployed scaffolds, compare flagged failures to independently observed incidents, and establish correlation.

claims_requiring_evidence.claim: The review dimensions reliably distinguish strong from weak responses across reviewers.
claims_requiring_evidence.evidence_needed: Inter-rater reliability testing with multiple independent reviewers.

claims_requiring_evidence.claim: The controlled category list is comprehensive.
claims_requiring_evidence.evidence_needed: Mapping against external failure-mode literature or incident taxonomies.

buyer_misread_risks: Schema could be mistaken for a finished, validated test suite rather than a generation template.
The phrase 'Hardened Schema Packet' could read as a security/compliance claim.
Categories near HR, clinical, security, or cross-cultural use cases could be misread as domain-certified tooling.
The AI-generation request section could undercut buyer trust if not framed as a disclosed internal drafting process.

language_risk_flags.fix: Use Structured or Refined in buyer-facing material.
Use structured internal review checklist in buyer-facing wording.

language_risk_flags.risk: Can imply safety certification if quoted out of context.

safe_buyer_facing_wording: SpecShift's internal review process uses a structured schema to generate synthetic adversarial test scenarios that probe how AI-system scaffolds handle specific failure patterns, including overclaiming, missing uncertainty boundaries, and unclear handoffs. This is a draft internal tool, not an industry benchmark, certification, or compliance product, and results from it should not be read as a guarantee of real-world performance.
safe_technical_wording: This schema defines a controlled-vocabulary, fixed-field structure for generating an internal adversarial test suite. It enforces structural properties such as case uniqueness, controlled categories, mandatory pass/fail conditions, and case-level failure columns, but does not itself constitute a validated evaluation harness.
internal_only_wording: Working schema for v0.5 suite generation. Status: pre-generation, not yet instantiated into actual test cases. Categories and review dimensions are internally generated, not externally validated.
evidence_needed_for_stronger_claims: Generate the actual v0.5 suite and run it against at least one real scaffold to establish baseline signal.
Independent multi-rater scoring of generated cases to establish review reliability.
Cross-check the controlled category list against external failure-mode or incident taxonomies.
Resolve artifact-name security-connotation risk before any external-facing use.
Apply explicit draft/non-validated disclosure language anywhere the schema or outputs are shown externally.
final_verdict: The schema is structurally sound and ready to proceed to case generation, but not ready for buyer-facing use until artifact naming and disclosure language are cleaned up.
